title: fabric-hint.cjs: gate Signal B on import-state in-flight + drop sentinel branch
description: Currently fabric-hint.cjs Signal B (pending overflow → recommend fabric-review) fires unconditionally and interrupts a running fabric-import skill at pending count >= 10. Add an isImportInFlight(cwd) helper that reads .fabric/.import-state.json and short-circuits Signal B (only) when import is mid-run. Simultaneously dismantle the sentinel-based recommendation branch (IMPORT_REQUESTED_SENTINEL_FILE, isImportRequestedSentinelPresent, makeImportSentinelResult, the sentinelPresent main() short-circuit, and any related exports/CONSTANTS surface) since the entire sentinel mechanism is being retired in TASK-003 + TASK-004. Maintain the never-block invariant.

criteria: isImportInFlight(cwd) helper exists and is the single decision point: returns false when .fabric/.import-state.json is missing, JSON.parse fails, phase==='complete', or last_checkpoint_at is older than IMPORT_IN_FLIGHT_MAX_AGE_HOURS (=24, declared as a top-level const)
Signal B branch in decide() is short-circuited only when isImportInFlight(cwd) returns true; Signals A, C, D retain their existing behaviour byte-for-byte

All sentinel surface is removed from fabric-hint.cjs: no IMPORT_REQUESTED_SENTINEL_FILE constant, no isImportRequestedSentinelPresent function, no makeImportSentinelResult function, no sentinelPresent short-circuit in main(), no sentinel-related exports, and no sentinel mention in CONSTANTS export object

Never-block invariant holds: if reading or parsing .import-state.json throws unexpectedly, decide() never returns decision:'block' that would otherwise be skipped — i.e. helper returns false on any error path
Updated test file packages/cli/__tests__/fabric-hint.test.ts adds the new gating cases (5 listed below) and removes all prior sentinel-specific tests; all other pre-existing tests pass unchanged
Hook contract surface (decide()/main() return shape) is unchanged for non-import-in-flight paths
convergence.definition_of_done: Running fabric-import past 10 pending entries no longer triggers a Stop-hook block; the sentinel mechanism is fully gone from fabric-hint.cjs.

files.target: decide() Signal B branch + main() sentinel short-circuit + sentinel constants/helpers/exports
files.change: Add top-level const `IMPORT_IN_FLIGHT_MAX_AGE_HOURS = 24` and helper `isImportInFlight(cwd)` (5 documented short-circuit cases). Wrap Signal B emit-check with `if (isImportInFlight(cwd)) skip Signal B`. Remove IMPORT_REQUESTED_SENTINEL_FILE, isImportRequestedSentinelPresent, makeImportSentinelResult, sentinelPresent main() branch, and all sentinel-related exports / CONSTANTS keys.

files.target: test suites for Signal B + sentinel removal
files.change: Add 5 new tests for the isImportInFlight gate; delete all sentinel-related tests; ensure baseline Signals A/C/D tests stay unchanged.

implementation: 1. Read packages/cli/templates/hooks/fabric-hint.cjs end-to-end to map: (a) decide() Signal B emit point, (b) main() sentinelPresent short-circuit, (c) all sentinel-related identifiers (constant, helper, factory, exports, CONSTANTS keys).
2. Add top-level constant `const IMPORT_IN_FLIGHT_MAX_AGE_HOURS = 24;` near the other DEFAULT_* constants.

3. Implement helper `function isImportInFlight(projectRoot) { ... }`: read `.fabric/.import-state.json`; on missing/parse-error return false; if `phase === 'complete'` return false; compute age from `last_checkpoint_at` (ISO string) — if missing or > IMPORT_IN_FLIGHT_MAX_AGE_HOURS hours old return false; otherwise return true.
4. In decide() at Signal B detection, add `if (isImportInFlight(projectRoot)) return /* skip B path */;` BEFORE the Signal B emit; do not touch Signals A, C, D.

5. Delete IMPORT_REQUESTED_SENTINEL_FILE constant, isImportRequestedSentinelPresent function, makeImportSentinelResult function. Remove the sentinelPresent block from main(). Remove sentinel keys from any exported CONSTANTS object and any module.exports surface.

6. Update packages/cli/__tests__/fabric-hint.test.ts: add the 5 new cases (state.json missing → B fires; phase=1/2/3 + fresh checkpoint → B silent; phase=complete → B fires; checkpoint > 24h ago → B fires; malformed JSON → B fires). Delete all tests referencing the removed sentinel identifiers.
7. Run `pnpm --filter @fabric/cli test -- fabric-hint` and confirm all suites pass.

manual_checks: Run fabric-import in a fixture project; confirm pending count crossing 10 does NOT trigger Stop-hook block
Manually corrupt .fabric/.import-state.json to invalid JSON; confirm hook still emits Signal B (never-block holds)
success_metrics: Signal B suppression triggers exactly when isImportInFlight()==true (5 unit cases cover the truth table)
Zero references to sentinel identifiers remain (grep IMPORT_REQUESTED_SENTINEL_FILE in fabric-hint.cjs returns 0)

regression: All non-Signal-B and non-sentinel tests in packages/cli/__tests__/fabric-hint.test.ts pass unchanged
decide() output shape for Signals A/C/D is byte-identical to pre-change for all existing fixtures
Other hooks that may import from fabric-hint.cjs (if any) continue to work — confirm via grep before merge

reference.pattern: Read-only state-file probe with never-block fallback (mirrors existing _readConfigNumber pattern in this same file)
reference.examples: See _readConfigNumber (around line 681) and readUnderseedThreshold (around line 746) for the established try/catch + default pattern; isImportInFlight follows the same defensive shape.

rationale.chosen_approach: Reuse the existing .fabric/.import-state.json (already written by fabric-import skill checkpoints) as the in-flight signal — zero new state, zero new contract. Hard-code the 24h TTL because 'config knob for stale-state timeout' is YAGNI and adds discoverability burden.
alternatives_considered: Add a dedicated .fabric/.import-running marker file written/cleared by fabric-import — rejected: doubles the state-file count and risks orphaned markers on crash
Make TTL configurable via fabric-config.json — rejected: YAGNI per design lock; 24h is a sensible upper bound for any real import session
Silence all signals (A/B/C/D) during import — rejected: A (archive opportunity) and D (maintenance) are independent and worth surfacing
decision_factors: Reuse existing state file (no new contract)
Maintain never-block invariant (defensive on every error path)
Coordinated removal of sentinel mechanism keeps codebase consistent
rationale.tradeoffs: If a user genuinely abandons an import for >24h, signal B will fire and may interrupt a fresh resume; acceptable because the user explicitly resumed a stale session.

risks.description: Other modules (or dogfooded user configs) import the removed sentinel identifiers
risks.mitigation: Grep the entire repo for IMPORT_REQUESTED_SENTINEL_FILE and isImportRequestedSentinelPresent before removing; coordinate with TASK-003 and TASK-004 which also drop these references
risks.fallback: Re-export a no-op stub temporarily if any external consumer is found

risks.description: last_checkpoint_at field name or format differs from assumption (ISO string vs epoch ms)
risks.mitigation: Inspect the actual schema/writer for .fabric/.import-state.json (in fabric-import skill code) before implementing; treat unparseable timestamps as stale (return false)
risks.fallback: Defensive Date.parse() with NaN check returning false (preserves never-block)

commit.message_template: fix(hooks): gate fabric-hint Signal B on import-state in-flight + drop sentinel
